column2: An employee owns the phone but uses it for company email and apps. Why is this harder to secure than a fully company-owned device?
column3: It mixes personal ownership/use with company data and requirements, so protecting and removing corporate data is more complicated.
column4:
Why

BYOD devices are both personal and work devices. That creates policy, privacy, and data-protection challenges.

Exam Trap

The ownership model matters. Do not treat BYOD as if the company has the same control it has over a corporate laptop.

column2: A company buys and controls a phone, but allows the employee to use it personally. Which ownership model best matches this?
column4:
Why

COPE means the device is corporate-owned but personally enabled. The organization keeps strong control while permitting personal use.

Related Cards

BYOD starts with employee ownership. CYOD is similar to COPE but gives the user a choice from approved devices.
